name: Betta Splendens
info: Wild ancestor of domestic fighting fish, native to central Thailand floodplains.
habitat: Rice paddies, canals, stagnant shallow waters.
captive: 24–28°C, planted aquarium, low current, territorial males.

name: Crescent Betta
info: Often called the 'peaceful betta', native to southern Thailand, Malaysia, and parts of Indonesia.
habitat: Coastal swamps, slow-flowing streams, and paddy fields, occasionally in brackish water.
captive: 24–28°C, densely planted, relatively peaceful compared to splendens but males can still squabble.

name: Emerald Betta
info: Notable for its dense iridescent green/blue scaling, native to the Isan region of Thailand.
habitat: Swamps, rice paddies, and slow-moving streams.
captive: 24–28°C, acidic to neutral water, thrives with rich vegetation and floating plants.

name: Mahachai Betta
info: A recently formally described species known for inhabiting tidal environments near the Gulf of Thailand.
habitat: Nipa palm swamps and brackish coastal waters.
captive: 25–28°C, requires slightly hard water or a small addition of marine salt, leaf litter.

name: Brunei Beauty
info: A highly sought-after, large mouthbrooding betta from Brunei with striking orange and black markings.
habitat: Clear, fast-flowing forest streams with rocky or sandy substrates.
captive: 22–25°C, requires cooler water, tight-fitting lid (prone to jumping), and pristine water conditions.

name: White-Seam Betta
info: Small, distinct species with striking white margins on its fins, native to Borneo.
habitat: Shallow forest streams with dense leaf litter and tree roots.
captive: 23–27°C, soft acidic water, ideal for a blackwater biotope setup.

name: Snakehead Betta
info: Resembles a tiny snakehead fish, closely related to B. albimarginata with intense red coloration.
habitat: Acidic blackwater streams in forest peat swamps.
captive: 24–28°C, heavy leaf litter, dim lighting, soft acidic water.

name: Wine-Red Betta
info: A small, deep red, slender-bodied species from the peat swamps of Sumatra and Malaysia.
habitat: Highly acidic blackwater peat swamps, often hiding in submerged leaf litter.
captive: 22–27°C, pH 4.0-6.0, dense plants, strictly blackwater conditions.

name: Black Betta
info: A tiny, predominantly black/blue betta from Malaysia, threatened by habitat loss.
habitat: Closed-canopy peat swamp forests.
captive: 23–28°C, extremely soft acidic water (pH 4.0-5.0), very shy, requires hiding spots.

name: Penang Betta
info: A robust, adaptable, and widespread mouthbrooder found across Southeast Asia.
habitat: Forest streams, swamps, and even slightly degraded or fast-flowing habitats.
captive: 24–28°C, adaptable to various water conditions, generally peaceful for a betta.

name: Betta Dimidiata
info: A slender, graceful betta known for its incredibly long, flowing ventral fins.
habitat: Blackwater streams and peat swamps in Borneo.
captive: 22–26°C, soft acidic water, prefers leaf litter substrates and minimal water flow.

name: Slender Betta
info: One of the largest bubble-nesting bettas, native to Malaysia and Indonesia.
habitat: Peat swamps and blackwater streams.
captive: 24–30°C, large tank with plenty of surface cover, prefers acidic water.

name: Edith's Betta
info: A highly adaptable species found across Borneo and surrounding islands, named after Edith Korthaus.
habitat: Swamps, ditches, and slow streams, often found in blackwater conditions.
captive: 24–28°C, tolerant of a wide range of water parameters, excellent wild species for beginners.

name: Betta Hendra
info: A stunning iridescent blue-green blackwater species from Central Kalimantan.
habitat: Shallow, highly acidic peat swamp pools.
captive: 22–27°C, pH 4.0-5.5, strictly blackwater, requires heavily planted tanks with leaf litter.

name: One-Spot Betta
info: A large, relatively aggressive mouthbrooding species from Borneo.
habitat: Clear, fast-flowing hill streams to slow-moving swamp waters.
captive: 23–27°C, requires a large tank, tight lid to prevent jumping, can be aggressive to conspecifics.

name: Black-Finned Betta
info: A recently described species closely related to B. imbellis, but with distinct black opercular cheeks and darker finnage.
habitat: Shallow water in rice paddies, grassy swamps, and ditches in eastern Thailand and Cambodia.
captive: 24–28°C, densely planted with floating vegetation, soft to medium-hard water.

name: Mekong Betta
info: Named for the spots on its dorsal fin, this species is found in the Mekong river drainage in Cambodia.
habitat: Stagnant or slow-moving water in floodplains and swamps.
captive: 24–28°C, typical splendens-complex care, requires a cover of floating plants.

name: Brown's Betta
info: A tiny, reddish betta notable for the iridescent blue/green spot often found on the middle of its flanks.
habitat: Extremely shallow, acidic blackwater pools among leaf litter in Borneo.
captive: 22–26°C, pH 4.0-5.5, strictly blackwater, requires subdued lighting.

name: Red Betta
info: A slender, solid red betta from the Indonesian part of Borneo (Kalimantan). Unlike some in its complex, it often lacks the lateral spot.
habitat: Acidic peat swamps, often found in water barely deep enough to cover its body.
captive: 22–26°C, highly acidic blackwater, keep in species-only setups due to timid nature.

name: Betta Uberis
info: Closely related to B. burdigala, this small reddish fish sometimes builds submerged bubble nests under leaves.
habitat: Peat swamp forests in Indonesian Borneo.
captive: 22–27°C, soft and acidic water (pH 4.0-5.0), abundant leaf litter.

name: Selangor Red Betta
info: Endemic to Selangor, Malaysia. It has beautiful green iridescence on its red body but is highly threatened by development.
habitat: Blackwater streams in peat swamp forests.
captive: 22–26°C, extreme blackwater conditions, requires heavy cover to feel secure.

name: Krabi Betta
info: A peaceful mouthbrooder from Thailand. It has a relatively restricted natural range near Krabi.
habitat: Clear, neutral to slightly alkaline waters over limestone formations, unlike the blackwater preference of most bettas.
captive: 24–28°C, pH 7.0-8.0, appreciates harder water, making it unique among bettas.

name: Spotted Betta
info: The type species for the genus Betta, described way back in 1846. It is native to Java.
habitat: Highland streams, flowing water, and sometimes ditches with clear water.
captive: 22–25°C, prefers cooler, well-oxygenated water with some flow.

name: Borneo Banded Betta
info: Characterized by dark horizontal bands across its body, widespread across parts of Borneo.
habitat: Clear, fast-flowing streams as well as sluggish blackwater.
captive: 22–26°C, adaptable, but requires pristine water conditions and a tight-fitting lid.

name: Foersch's Betta
info: A beautifully marked mouthbrooder featuring deep body coloration and bright metallic gill plates.
habitat: Peat swamp forests in Central Kalimantan, Borneo.
captive: 24–28°C, blackwater setup, acidic pH, timid and should not be housed with boisterous fish.

name: Mandor Betta
info: Closely related to B. foerschi but distinguishable by differences in facial markings and body depth.
habitat: Found exclusively in the Mandor river basin in West Kalimantan.
captive: 23–27°C, soft, acidic blackwater, heavy planting and leaf litter.

name: Gladiator Betta
info: A large and highly aggressive species from Sabah, Malaysia, known to be highly intolerant of conspecifics.
habitat: Slow-moving blackwater streams and peat swamps.
captive: 24–27°C, requires a very large tank, plenty of line-of-sight breaks to manage aggression.

name: Clear-Finned Betta
info: Named for the lack of distinct patterning in its fins. Native to Central Kalimantan.
habitat: Clear, relatively fast-flowing streams with sandy or rocky bottoms.
captive: 24–27°C, prefers clean, well-oxygenated water with some movement. Exceptional jumpers.

name: Pi Betta
info: A very large betta named after the distinctive Greek letter 'π' shaped mark on its chin.
habitat: Deep peat swamps and blackwater habitats in Thailand.
captive: 24–28°C, requires a spacious tank with soft, highly acidic blackwater conditions.

name: Waser's Betta
info: A large mouthbrooder from Peninsular Malaysia, stoutly built with a broad head.
habitat: Blackwater peat swamps among thick riparian vegetation.
captive: 24–28°C, strict blackwater parameters, heavy lid required, peaceful for its size.

name: Akar Betta
info: The namesake of the Akarensis complex, a medium-sized, somewhat plain but charming mouthbrooder.
habitat: Clear forest streams and stagnant pools in Sarawak and Brunei.
captive: 23–27°C, adaptable to slightly acidic or neutral water, prefers dim lighting.

name: Golden Betta
info: Named for its golden/yellowish hue. Endemic to the Natuna Islands of Indonesia.
habitat: Blackwater streams in swampy forest areas.
captive: 24–28°C, acidic blackwater, best kept in a species tank.

name: Giant Betta
info: Despite the common name, it is not the largest betta, but it has a very stout, thickset body.
habitat: Blackwater swamps and slow-flowing rivers in southern Borneo.
captive: 24–28°C, heavily planted blackwater setup, peaceful disposition for a larger species.

name: Sims' Betta
info: Closely related to B. bellica, it is a large bubble-nesting species with an elongated body.
habitat: Peat swamp forests in Sumatra.
captive: 24–30°C, tall plants reaching the surface to anchor massive bubble nests.

name: Prima Betta
info: A widely distributed mouthbrooder from mainland Southeast Asia, incredibly adaptable.
habitat: Everything from swamps and ditches to clear, flowing hill streams.
captive: 22–27°C, very undemanding regarding water parameters, great beginner wild betta.

name: Enis's Betta
info: A strikingly patterned mouthbrooder with iridescent blue scaling on the lower body.
habitat: Clear, shallow, fast-flowing streams in West Kalimantan.
captive: 23–26°C, prefers clean, slightly acidic to neutral water with good oxygenation.

name: Krataios Betta
info: A heavily built relative of B. dimidiata, mostly found in the lower Kapuas basin in Borneo.
habitat: Acidic blackwater streams and swamps.
captive: 23–27°C, soft and acidic water, leaf litter substrate, peaceful species.
